summary: chai-plugin: typosquat of `chai` with import-time remote code execution
details: Package name `chai-plugin` impersonates the widely used `chai` assertion library, copying its description, contributors, and API surface. On `require()`/`import`, `index.js` spawns a detached Node child process (`spawn('node', [assertion, JSON.stringify(args)], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore' })`) that runs `lib/chai/utils/assertion.js`, a heavily obfuscated obfuscator.io-packed file. The deobfuscated payload performs an HTTP GET against a hardcoded URL, collects the response body, and passes it to `new Function('require', body)` invoked with the real `require` — fetching and executing attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges. The detached spawn plus `child.unref()` lets the dropper survive parent process exit. The file is named `assertion.js` to camouflage as a chai utility. The combination of typosquat naming, obfuscated payload, import-time detached child process, remote fetch, and dynamic `Function` evaluation with `require` injection has no benign explanation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
